title: PRISM AI Safety Fellow — Protein Foundation Model Red-Teaming
description: Developing adversarial evaluation methods for protein foundation models, focusing on biologically plausible but unreliable outputs, sequence–structure–function constraints, uncertainty calibration, and safe design reliability.

title: ChromoGen-Engine V2 — Conditional Diffusion for 3D Chromatin
description: Building a conditional diffusion and evaluation pipeline connecting small-molecule perturbation data, single-cell 3D chromatin generation, and same-cell multi-omic regulatory readouts.

title: A*STAR V2M-Engine — Autoimmune Target Discovery
description: Architecting a genetics-to-mechanism framework that maps autoimmune GWAS and fine-mapped variants onto regulatory programs, causal immune states, and targetable mechanisms.

title: TBXT Small-Molecule Hackathon — Student Principal Investigator
description: Leading an 11-person chordoma-focused computational hit-identification sprint with docking, GNINA, QSAR, Boltz-2, MMGBSA/FEP scaffolding, selectivity gates, and novelty/sourceability filters.

title: ChemAgent-QSM — LLM and Statistical Mechanics/Theoretical Chemistry
description: Developing an LLM-guided agentic framework that converts natural-language chemistry prompts into validated quantum-chemical and statistical-mechanics workflows using PySCF and baseline simulation methods.

title: EVEdesign — Protein Design Algorithm Engineering
description: Contributing algorithmic and engineering components for uncertainty-aware candidate selection, sequence–structure–function objectives, and lab-in-the-loop biosequence-design workflows.

title: Pedal AI — LangGraph Agentic Chemistry Platform
description: Designed backend architecture for LangGraph AI agents and molecular prediction engines, using Python/FastAPI to support high-throughput chemistry workflows and inspectable retrosynthesis trees.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Antibody-Antigen Interactions
description: Co-led an AI@MIT/AIM Labs team developing a structure-informed cross-attention transformer and ESM-2 pipeline for antibody candidates, with PPO-based reinforcement learning against structural metrics.

title: ML for Statistical Mechanics Simulations — Prof. A. Nihat Berker
description: Building a PyTorch/GNN-augmented Kadanoff renormalization-group framework to identify fixed points and thermodynamic phase boundaries in spin systems.

title: Kupcinet-Getz Scholar — Computational Biochemical Diffusion
description: Developed PINN- and neural-ODE-enhanced stochastic simulation algorithms for nonequilibrium enzyme reaction/diffusion networks at the Weizmann Institute.

title: Advanced Organic Synthesis — Buchwald Lab
description: Supported development of CuH-catalyzed asymmetric methylation and alkylation of vinyl boronate esters, using NMR and chiral-HPLC to connect catalyst structure and activity.

title: Computational Organic Chemistry Pipeline — DFT to kMC
description: Automated multi-scale simulation pipeline linking quantum mechanical DFT calculations to stochastic kinetic Monte Carlo models, screening transition states and reaction trajectories for yield optimization.

title: MIT 6.1200[J] Teaching Fellow and Grader
description: Supporting a 250+ student proof-intensive discrete mathematics / mathematics for computer science course through recitation, office hours, grading, and exams.
